questions.question: 中文导读：How does a ViT turn an image into a sequence of tokens?
options: 中文选项：Flattens the entire image into one vector
中文选项：Applies a conv with kernel_size = stride = patch_size to split the image into non-overlapping patches and linearly project each to a token embedding in one operation
中文选项：Runs a CNN first and uses its feature map as tokens
中文选项：Sorts pixel intensities
questions.explanation: 中文解析：The first layer of a ViT is a conv with kernel_size = stride = 16, turning a 224x224 image into a 14x14 grid of 16x16 patches, each projected to dim=768. That single conv does both patchification and projection. The result is 196 tokens that a standard transformer encoder can process.

questions.question: 中文导读：What is the [CLS] token in ViT and why is it needed?
options: 中文选项：A learned vector prepended to the token sequence; after N transformer layers its output aggregates the whole image and feeds the classifier head
中文选项：A special pixel value
中文选项：A placeholder for missing data
中文选项：A dropout mask
questions.explanation: 中文解析：ViT follows BERT's convention: prepend a learned vector (the CLS token) to the patch sequence. Self-attention lets every token see every other token, so the CLS token aggregates global information from all patches. The classifier reads only the CLS output. Alternative designs use average-pooling over all patch tokens (used by ConvNeXt and many post-ViT models).

questions.question: 中文导读：Why did the original ViT paper need JFT-300M pretraining to beat ResNet, and why does DeiT not?
options: 中文选项：ViT is broken without JFT-300M

中文选项：ViT has weaker inductive biases than CNNs; without enough data it overfits. DeiT showed that with strong augmentation (RandAugment, Mixup, CutMix), stochastic depth, and optionally CNN distillation, ViTs train fine on ImageNet-1k alone
中文选项：DeiT uses different attention
中文选项：The ViT paper was wrong
questions.explanation: 中文解析：ViT's lack of inductive bias (no locality, no translation equivariance baked in) means the network has to learn these from data. With 1M ImageNet images that is hard; with 300M JFT images it is easy. DeiT replaced the data with training-recipe improvements: heavy augmentation, stochastic depth, and distillation from a CNN teacher, all of which compensate for the lack of priors.

questions.question: 中文导读：Pre-LayerNorm (`x = x + sublayer(LN(x))`) vs post-LayerNorm (`x = LN(x + sublayer(x))`) — which is used in modern transformers and why?
options: 中文选项：Post-LN; it is simpler
中文选项：Pre-LN; it trains deeper networks stably without learning-rate warmup and avoids the gradient instabilities that post-LN suffers past about 6 layers
中文选项：They are identical
中文选项：Neither; modern transformers skip LayerNorm
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Post-LN was the original transformer (2017) and required careful warmup + small learning rates past 6-8 layers. Pre-LN (Xiong et al., 2020; used by ViT, GPT-2+, every modern LLM) is numerically more stable: the residual stream accumulates without passing through LN, and each sublayer operates on a normalised input. Every transformer deeper than 12 layers uses pre-LN.

questions.question: 中文导读：Swin Transformer introduces windowed attention. What problem does it solve?
options: 中文选项：Vanishing gradients

中文选项：Full attention has O((H*W)^2) cost; Swin restricts attention to local windows of 7x7 patches, reducing cost to O(H*W * window^2). Alternating blocks shift the window by half its size so information eventually mixes across the image
中文选项：BatchNorm not supported
中文选项：Overfitting to texture
questions.explanation: 中文解析：A 224x224 image with 4x4 patches has 56*56 = 3136 tokens; full attention is 3136^2 = ~10M pairs per layer. Swin attends only within 7x7 windows, giving 49^2 = 2401 pairs per window (plus the window count). Shifted windows in alternating blocks mix information across windows over a few layers. This brings a CNN-like locality prior back to the transformer without giving up attention.
